meta.title: Fatima Mohsin Khan — Software Engineer & Product Designer
meta.description: Fatima Mohsin Khan builds interfaces grounded in psychology and ships the code behind them. LUMS CS '26. Portfolio, projects, and an honest AI twin.

hero.name: Fatima Mohsin Khan
hero.dedication: The life and girlfriend of Musa
hero.tagline: I design with psychology. I ship with code.
hero.sub: LUMS CS '26 — building interfaces that respect how people actually think.

timeline.title: First semester at LUMS
timeline.body: Started BS Computer Science, unsure whether I'd end up in engineering or design.

timeline.title: First Dean's Honor List
timeline.body: Fall 2023. Also the semester I took Intro to Visual Design almost by accident.

timeline.title: Software engineering internship
timeline.body: Joined Lumen Loop for the summer, building full-stack scheduling features for campus societies.

timeline.title: Design Lead, LUMS Design Collective
timeline.body: Started running critique sessions and workshops for the campus design community.

timeline.title: Product design internship
timeline.body: Joined Notch Studio, running user research and building a client design system.

timeline.title: Graduated LUMS
timeline.body: Finished with a second Dean's Honor List and Sahulat, my capstone, already live with real users.

projects.title: Rasta
projects.oneLiner: An accessibility-first wayfinding app for the LUMS campus.
projects.problem: A visually impaired classmate couldn't use the existing campus map with a screen reader.
projects.approach: Built full keyboard navigation, ARIA-labeled route steps, high-contrast mode, and an audio building-description mode.
impact: Used by roughly 80 students in its first semester
Full keyboard and screen-reader navigation across every route
Won 1st place, Accessibility Tech track, Indus Valley Hackathon
projects.lensNote: Leaned on clear, consistent feedback cues so non-visual navigation never left a user guessing what happened.

projects.title: Alfaaz
projects.oneLiner: An Urdu-language tool that summarizes long news articles into three sentences.
projects.problem: Most summarization tools handle English well and treat Urdu as an afterthought.
projects.approach: Fine-tuned a multilingual transformer model with custom Urdu preprocessing, served through a FastAPI backend.
impact: Tested on a self-built 50-article held-out set
projects.lensNote: Kept summaries to exactly three sentences on purpose, respecting how much a reader will actually scan before moving on.

projects.title: Marrow
projects.oneLiner: A shared component library so I'd stop rebuilding the same button every semester.
projects.problem: Every new project meant rebuilding the same buttons, modals, and forms from scratch.
projects.approach: Built 25+ accessible React components with Storybook docs, a shared design-token system, and a synced Figma library.
impact: Used across 3 of my own other projects
One shared token system replacing inconsistent one-off styles
projects.lensNote: Standardized interaction patterns using the Von Restorff effect deliberately, so the one button that matters on a screen still stands out.

projects.title: Sahulat
projects.oneLiner: A peer-tutoring marketplace connecting LUMS students, launched to real users before graduation.
projects.problem: Students needing help in a course had no easy way to find peers who'd already done well in it.
projects.approach: Designed and built a two-sided marketplace with scheduling, reviews, and Stripe-based session payments.
impact: Launched to my own graduating cohort before I graduated
projects.lensNote: Reduced the booking flow to the fewest possible taps, treating every extra screen as a chance to lose someone who was already unsure.

philosophy.intro: I don't separate design taste from engineering judgment. Every layout or flow decision I make traces back to a specific idea about attention, memory, or motivation. Naming the principle keeps me honest about whether it actually worked.

principles.name: Hick's Law
principles.claim: More choices on screen means slower decisions, so I cut options before I add polish.

principles.name: Fitts's Law
principles.claim: The more often an action matters, the bigger and closer its target should be.

principles.name: Von Restorff effect
principles.claim: One item that looks different gets remembered; everything else should look calm.

skills.note: I go deep where a project demanded it, not evenly everywhere. Ask me what I haven't done — I'll tell you straight.

offscreen.intro: The parts of the timeline that didn't make it into a project card.
facts: I can recite most of Nielsen Norman Group's usability heuristics from memory.
I keep a running note of UI patterns that annoyed me that day, past 200 entries now.
I read music before I learned to code, and I still think in rhythm when I think about pacing.
My best debugging happens in the LUMS library reading room around 11pm.
I've never shipped a mobile app to an app store, and it bothers me more than it should.
I organized a 60-person campus workshop and was more nervous presenting it than defending my capstone.

values.name: Specific over abstract
values.body: Every project started with one real person's real complaint.

values.name: Communicate early, then build
values.body: I over-align at the start so I can go quiet and just ship.

values.name: Name the principle
values.body: If I can't say why a decision helps, it's still a guess.

contact.headline: Let's talk
contact.body: I'm available now, open to relocating, and open to remote or hybrid roles. Reach out and I'll get back to you directly.
contact.closing: Thanks for scrolling this far. Say hello.

chat.title: Fatima's AI twin
chat.subtitle: Grounded in her real portfolio. Honest by design.
chat.welcome: Hi, I'm Fatima's AI twin. Ask me about her projects, her stack, or her design process. I'll also tell you straight when something's outside what she's actually done.
chips: Is she more engineer or designer?
How does she work in a team?
chat.disclaimer: This AI may summarize loosely. Verify anything specific with the human Fatima.

footer.line: Designed & built from scratch — three.js, GSAP, and an unreasonable love of easing curves.

resume.headline: Fatima Mohsin Khan
resume.summary: LUMS CS '26 graduate building interfaces grounded in psychology and shipping the full-stack code behind them.

Cut event-listing page load time from 2.8s to 1.4s by batching API calls

Designed a 40-component design system in Figma, replacing three inconsistent UI patterns

Built end-to-end with Next.js, PostgreSQL, and Stripe payments

skills.design: Figma, user research, usability testing, design systems, accessibility
